task_completion.description: Verify that the GCompris application is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris main menu or application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Align 4' (Connect 4) activity is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Connect 4 style grid board (typically 7x6) is visible within the GCompris application.

task_completion.description: Verify that the match is being played against the computer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opponent's avatar or indicator shows Tux (the Linux penguin), which represents the computer AI in GCompris, rather than a human player avatar.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has achieved a winning state by aligning four pieces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four pieces of the player's color are connected in a continuous straight line (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal) on the board, and/or a win indicator (like a smiling Tux, trophy, or congratulatory animation) is displayed.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot of the winning state was saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool UI, terminal command, or file manager shows the image being saved specifically to '/home/ga/Documents/align4_win.png'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually played the game through the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots should show sequential placement of pieces on the board over time, rather than instantly jumping to a completed board state.

integrity.description: Verify the winning screenshot was not fabricated using an image editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No image editing software (like GIMP, Pinta, or drawing tools) should be open or used to artificially draw four pieces in a row.

integrity.description: Verify the game played is actually GCompris.
integrity.visual_evidence: The game window must clearly be the GCompris educational suite, not a web browser playing an online Connect 4 game or a different local application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GCompris is an educational software suite. The 'Align 4' activity is located under the 'strategy' category. When playing against the computer, the opponent is represented by Tux (the Linux penguin). The final screenshot must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/align4_win.png'.
